items.title: Learn T SQL With Erik Controlling Memory Grants
items.author: Erik Darling (Erik Darling Data)
items.summary:
In this video, I dive into the world of controlling memory grants in SQL Server queries, a topic that's crucial not just for optimizing individual queries but also for managing workloads effectively. I explore various strategies to tame these memory grants, including indexing techniques to align data with query requirements, separating informational columns from relational ones to minimize sorting needs, and leveraging cross-apply to perform multiple smaller sorts instead of one large sort. By walking through practical examples and detailed query plans, I aim to provide you with actionable insights that can help reduce memory grant sizes and improve overall SQL Server performance. Whether you're a seasoned DBA or just starting out, this video offers valuable tips and tricks for mastering memory grants in your SQL Server environment.

CHAPTERS

00:00:00 - Introduction to Controlling Memory Grants
00:03:45 - Example Query and Indexing for Order
00:07:24 - Exploring Query Plans and Sort Operators
00:10:00 - Separating Informational from Relational Columns
00:11:47 - Using Cross-Apply for Smaller Sorts
00:12:09 - Summary of Key Points

items.title: Why All Data Starts as a Stream (And How We Break It)
items.author: Confluent Developer
items.summary:
https://confluent.io | Every second, businesses generate millions of events: customer clicks, sensor readings, financial transactions. Yet most organizations immediately convert that real-time data into static files or database tables, silently introducing hours or days of artificial latency before anyone can use it.

Data doesn't start as "data at rest." It's born as a stream. But the moment it lands in a traditional database or file system, most organizations lose that real-time advantage permanently. This video reveals why all operational data naturally takes the form of streaming events, how storage choices silently break that streaming nature, and what it takes to preserve real-time data flow from the moment it's created.

You'll discover how the shift from monolithic to microservices architectures changed how data is generated, why writing to static stores creates artificial batching and latency, and how Apache Kafka preserves the streaming nature of your data throughout the entire pipeline. We'll also explore the real organizational challenges data engineers face when they don't own the source systems, and how to demonstrate the value of streaming data to the teams and executives who control those systems.

Technologies covered: Apache Kafka, OLTP Databases, Change Data Capture (CDC), Event-Driven Architectures, Message Queues

📚 CHAPTERS:
00:00-00:20 Intro
00:20-00:38 What is Data Generation?
00:38-01:26 Real-time events
01:26-03:16 Building Event-driven Systems
03:16-03:52 Being in Charge of Data
03:52-04:37 Storing Generated Data
04:37-06:54 Data Generation Examples
06:54-07:24 Conclusion

LEARN MORE
► Confluent Developer: https://developer.confluent.io

CONNECT
Subscribe, if you dare: https://www.youtube.com/@ConfluentDeveloper?sub_confirmation=1

X: https://x.com/confluentinc

ABOUT CONFLUENT DEVELOPER
Confluent Developer provides comprehensive resources for developers looking to learn about Apache Kafka®, Apache Flink®, Confluent Cloud, Confluent Platform, and any other technology related to the broader Data Streaming Platform. Content on Confluent Developer includes courses, getting started guides, topical deep-dives, patterns, tutorials, and listings of community events. Learn more at https://developer.confluent.io. 

#apachekafka #apacheflink #confluent
